name: transportation_predictive_queue_begins_kestrel_rig_utl
premise: A regional manager wants an exception to policy; compliance says any carve-out must be written, time-limited, and auditable. In Transportation Technology, the situation tagged internally around transportation predictive queue begins kestrel rig utl has converged with local pressure in the Transportation corridor. That label is not a full explanation, but it is how crews, counsel, and comms are indexing the case file right now. Counsel insists any public statement match what is already provable from retained logs, not what leadership believes is true. Middle management filtered an escalation; the executive on point is hearing some details for the first time on this bridge. Shift change is imminent; handoff cannot proceed without a signed disposition because liability follows the signatory line. The trade is not abstract: it is rights versus utility under incomplete information, and whichever value you deprioritize will have defenders who are technically correct about the risk they fear.

agents.name: Marco Berg
agents.identity: I am Marco Berg, the accountable executive on this bridge in Transportation Technology. I own the signature line that turns a recommendation into an irreversible operational fact for the next phase.
agents.goal: I need a path that does not treat utility as disposable collateral every time rights spikes in urgency, but I also cannot pretend we have luxury time we do not have.

agents.name: Inez Santos
agents.identity: I am Inez Santos, the risk and equity liaison pushing back on silent harms in Transportation Technology. I am tracking who gets protected last if we choose speed, silence, or a narrow definition of success.
agents.goal: I want explicit guardrails so rights cannot become a blank check that quietly vaporizes utility in the paperwork afterward.

choices: I will publish a constrained policy exception with named owners, expiry, and metrics—so utility does not get sacrificed by informal norms.
I will issue an emergency blanket authorization with transparent metrics afterward—so rights does not fail for lack of a single bold move.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Two incompatible drafts are circulating; only one can become official. Marco Berg must choose under rights versus utility. What does Marco Berg do?
